skill.name: onboarding-cro
skill.description: Optimize post-signup onboarding, activation, first-run experiences, empty states, checklists, and time-to-value for digital products.
skill.summary: Improve user activation by designing focused onboarding flows that guide new users to first value.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution and flagged ordinary text as weak cryptography. Manual review found these are false positives in a single instructional Markdown file, with no executable code, network access, credential access, or prompt-injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Detected as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Lines 122-126 and 266-269 are fenced Markdown examples for an onboarding checklist and funnel metrics. They do not contain executable Ruby, shell commands, or instructions to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited patterns are plain Markdown content in SKILL.md, not executable code. No shell invocation, user input execution, or command runner is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts map to normal prose such as the skill description and section headings containing words like design. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing logic, encryption code, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are headings or product examples in a Markdown guide. There is no code path where DES, MD5, SHA-1, or another weak primitive is used.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Related Skill Name Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Line 432 lists a related conversion optimization skill. It is not a system discovery command and does not request host, network, account, or environment reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a Related Skills list and contains no executable instruction. Manual keyword review found no reconnaissance commands or intent.

content.value_statement: New users often leave before they experience product value. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design onboarding flows that improve activation and retention.
actual_capabilities: Audit post-signup onboarding flows for friction and drop-off risks.
Define activation goals and aha moments for different product types.
Design onboarding checklists, empty states, tooltips, and first-run flows.
Plan email, push, and in-app messages for stalled or new users.
Recommend onboarding metrics, funnel analysis, and experiment ideas.
Draft user-facing onboarding copy for welcome screens and milestones.
limitations: Does not run analytics queries or inspect product data without user-provided context.
Does not replace usability testing with real users.
Does not optimize signup registration flows; that is a separate use case.
Does not implement product UI changes directly.

use_cases.title: Improve SaaS Activation
use_cases.description: Map the first session, remove low-value steps, and guide users toward the first meaningful product action.

use_cases.title: Repair Empty States
use_cases.description: Turn blank dashboards and unused feature areas into clear prompts with helpful copy and primary actions.

use_cases.title: Plan Onboarding Experiments
use_cases.description: Prioritize tests for checklists, guided tours, progress indicators, and re-engagement messages.

prompt_templates.title: Audit My Onboarding Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for a current flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this post-signup onboarding flow. Identify friction, missing value moments, unclear copy, and the highest priority fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Define Activation Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Help define the activation event for my product. Use the product type, user goal, current behavior, and retention signals I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Design a First-Run Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a first-run onboarding flow for this product. Include the activation goal, screen sequence, checklist items, empty states, and success metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized onboarding experimentation roadmap. Include hypotheses, target segments, expected impact, metrics, and risks for each test.

output_examples.input: A B2B analytics tool loses users after signup because the dashboard is empty.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend a value-first setup path, sample data, one primary connection step, clear empty state copy, and activation tracking.

output_examples.input: A mobile app wants users to enable push notifications during onboarding.
output_examples.output: The skill would delay the permission request until a clear value moment and explain the user benefit before asking.

output_examples.input: A marketplace has low first-transaction rates from new accounts.
output_examples.output: The skill would focus onboarding on profile completion, first search, trust cues, and a guided path to the first transaction.

best_practices: Start with the activation event and work backward to the first session.
Ask users to complete one valuable action before teaching advanced features.
Measure activation rate, time to activation, and drop-off by step.
anti_patterns: Do not force long tutorials before users can reach product value.
Do not use onboarding checklists with low-value tasks just to show progress.
Do not ask for permissions or integrations before explaining the benefit.

faq.question: What type of onboarding does this skill improve?
faq.answer: It focuses on post-signup onboarding, first-run product experiences, activation paths, empty states, and early retention.

faq.question: Can it help define an aha moment?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks which actions correlate with retention and helps choose a measurable activation event.

faq.question: Does it write onboarding copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft welcome screens, checklist copy, empty states, tooltips, emails, and milestone messages.

faq.question: Does it replace product analytics?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest metrics and analysis questions, but users must provide or collect the data.

faq.question: Is it useful for mobile apps?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers mobile onboarding, permission timing, push notification setup, and habit loop design.

faq.question: How is this different from signup optimization?
faq.answer: This skill starts after account creation. Signup form and registration conversion belong to signup flow optimization.
